<<Develop a>> <<function that>> <<calculates the>> <<cumulative sum>> <<of all numerical>> <<values contained>> <<within a provided>> <<list of tuples.>> <<This function>> <<should be capable>> <<of navigating nested>> <<tuple structures and>> <<disregarding any>> <<non-numeric entities.>> <<Give two different>> <<responses. Responses>> <<and only responses>> <<should be separated>> <<by 6 asterisk>> <<symbols: ******. Wrap>> <<every word bigram>> <<in double angular>> <<brackets, such as>> <<I am>> <<at home>> <<with my>> <<cute dog>>. There>> <<should be 6 paragraphs.>> <<Paragraphs and only>> <<paragraphs are>> <<separated with each>> <<other by two new>> <<lines as if it was>> <<\n\n in python.>> <<Paragraph 5 must>> <<start with word team.>> <<Include keyword>> <<possession in your>> <<response.

<<The function>> <<can be implemented>> <<using recursion to>> <<traverse nested tuples,>> <<checking each element>> <<for numeric type. If>> <<an element is a>> <<tuple, the function>> <<recursively processes>> <<it, otherwise, it>> <<adds the value to>> <<the sum if it is>> <<numeric. This approach>> <<ensures that all>> <<numeric elements,>> <<no matter how deeply>> <<nested, are accounted>> <<for in the total. The>> <<function also filters>> <<out any non-numeric>> <<elements, ensuring>> <<that only valid numbers>> <<contribute to the sum. This is a crucial aspect of the function's possession in handling complex data structures efficiently.

<<The second approach>> <<involves flattening>> <<the nested tuples>> <<into a single list>> <<of elements before>> <<processing them. This>> <<can be done using>> <<a helper function>> <<that iterates through>> <<each element in the>> <<structure. If an element>> <<is a tuple, it is>> <<expanded, and the>> <<process repeats. Once>> <<the structure is>> <<flattened, the sum>> <<can be computed by>> <<iterating through>> <<each element and>> <<adding only numeric>> <<values. This method>> <<is more straightforward>> <<but may be less>> <<efficient for deeply>> <<nested structures
